data.title: DAVOS: U.S. to Press Ahead With EU Trade Deal Despite Opposition, Bessent Says
data.description:
By Ed Frankl 




  Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said he expects to go ahead with the U.S.'s trade deal with the European Union, despite some EU lawmakers saying they might hold up the agreement over President Trump's comments over Greenland. 



  "We're in the midst of completing a very good trade deal for both sides, so the plan is to go ahead with the trade deal," Bessent said at the World Economics Forum in Davos, Switzerland. 



  Some European lawmakers have said they might put the implementation of the trade deal on hold after President Trump's threat of additional tariffs on eight European nations that didn't agree with his desire to acquire the Arctic territory. 



  Bessent stressed that the EU shouldn't retaliate to Trump's tariff threats. 



  "I would say exactly what I said after 'Liberation Day' last April when the president imposed tariff levels on the whole world. I tell everyone sit back, take a deep breath, do not retaliate," he said. 



  Despite the strain Trump's comments might place on its military alliance with Europe, Bessent said that the North Atlantic Treaty Organization was secure under Trump. 



  "I think that the Nato alliance is very safe now, and thanks to President Trump, it's never been more secure," he added. 





  Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 20, 2026 09:28 ET (14:28 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Niron Magnetics and FaitalPRO Unveil Sustainable Loudspeaker Designs at NAMM 2026 and ISE 2026
data.description:
Niron Magnetics and FaitalPRO Unveil Sustainable Loudspeaker Designs at NAMM 2026 and ISE 2026

Niron Magnetics and FaitalPRO announced the development of new professional audio loudspeakers featuring breakthrough Iron Nitride permanent magnets. These next-generation loudspeakers, which deliver strong acoustic performance while eliminating rare-earth dependencies, will be showcased at leading audio and audiovisual industry trade shows NAMM 2026 and ISE 2026.

The collaboration combines Niron Magnetics’ Iron Nitride permanent magnet technology with FaitalPRO’s loudspeaker engineering expertise to create a new professional audio solution that addresses both performance and sustainability requirements.

“This collaboration demonstrates that sustainable innovation doesn’t require performance compromises,” said Jonathan Rowntree, CEO of Niron Magnetics. “Our Iron Nitride permanent magnets enable speaker manufacturers to reduce dependence on rare-earth supply chains while delivering the acoustic quality expected in demanding professional audio applications. This project with FaitalPRO is just the beginning of demonstrating the real-world applications of our technology.”

As part of this collaboration, FaitalPRO has developed two professional audio loudspeakers, an 8 and 10-inch, specifically designed to integrate Iron Nitride magnet technology. The project successfully met its primary objective of demonstrating that rare-earth-free magnet solutions can fully meet and exceed the expectations in professional loudspeaker applications. This collaboration represents an important step toward a new generation of professional audio products in which performance and sustainability coexist naturally, without compromise.

This also comes at a time when demand for permanent magnets continues to grow across multiple industries. Permanent magnets play a critical role in audio transducers, and the availability of rare-earth-free solutions made from abundant materials such as iron and nitrogen represents an important opportunity to support a more sustainable and resilient supply chain.

Attendees will be able to experience the new FaitalPRO loudspeakers featuring Iron Nitride magnet technology at booth #17506 at NAMM 2026 and at booth 7K200 at ISE 2026.

About Niron Magnetics

Niron Magnetics is the world’s only producer of high-performance, rare-earth-free permanent magnets. The company’s Iron Nitride magnet technology delivers advanced permanent magnets that power essential devices across industrial, automotive, consumer electronics, and defense applications. Through its state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Minnesota, Niron Magnetics serves leading manufacturers who require reliable, domestically produced permanent magnets for their products. For more information on Niron Magnetics and its technology, please visit https://www.nironmagnetics.com/.

About FaitalPRO

FaitalPRO is an Italian manufacturer specializing in the design and production of high-performance loudspeakers and transducers for professional audio applications. With strong engineering expertise, an R&D-driven approach, and fully Made in Italy production, FaitalPRO is internationally recognized for the quality, reliability, and innovation of its products. The company develops solutions for PA systems, live sound, and fixed installations, with a growing focus on sustainable technologies and advanced materials. For more information, please visit https://faitalpro.com/highlights/sustainablesound/.

Media:
Antonella Eljach: antonella.eljach@deeptech.agency

data.title: Novo Nordisk, Aspect Biosystems to Develop Curative Medicines for Diabetes
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping 




  Novo Nordisk agreed to invest in Aspect Biosystems and provide research funding as the companies extend an existing partnership that will seek to develop curative medicines for diabetes. 



  The companies have partnered since 2023, when they agreed to collaborate on the development of disease-modifying treatments for diabetes and obesity. In an extension of that deal, they said Tuesday that Aspect has acquired rights to engineering technologies from Novo Nordisk and will lead development, manufacturing and commercialization of cellular medicines for diabetes. 



  Novo Nordisk will have the right to expand its role in later-stage development and commercialization, and will also be eligible to receive royalties and milestone payments on future product sales from Aspect. 



  "We have a continued focus on bringing innovation to people living with type 1 diabetes through internal and external innovation efforts," said Jacob Sten Petersen, senior vice president of global research at Novo Nordisk. 



  The agreement will see Novo Nordisk's cell therapy research, development, and manufacturing capabilities as well as expertise from the U.S. and Denmark integrated into Aspect's Canada-based platform. 



  The partnership seeks to progress cell therapies toward clinical development and potentially generate a functional cure for people living with diabetes, Petersen added. 



  Financial terms weren't disclosed. 





  Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 20, 2026 09:18 ET (14:18 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Leading East Coast Printing Partner Southeastern Announces Acquisition of Sunbelt Expanding its Dimensional and Structural Printing Capabilities
data.description:
Leading East Coast Printing Partner Southeastern Announces Acquisition of Sunbelt Expanding its Dimensional and Structural Printing Capabilities

This strategic move strengthens Southeastern’s integrated Print, Dimensional, Promo, and Fulfillment offerings for rapidly growing brands.

Southeastern, a top provider of print, fulfillment, and promotional products solutions, announced the acquisition of Sunbelt, including the related entities, Sunbelt Graphics and Sunbelt Dimensional. The Florida dimensional print company, established in 1970, will operate under Southeastern's umbrella as Sunbelt Dimensional.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260119170035/en/

Southeastern announces the acquisition of Sunbelt, adding dimensional print and packaging to its suite of marketing solutions.

The acquisition is a key step in Southeastern’s long-term growth strategy and further solidifies its position as a single-source partner for brands that require print, dimensional, promotional products, and fulfillment with state-of-the-art digital storefront technology under one roof.

“The future of print isn’t just ink on paper—it’s the entire physical experience of a brand and how they leverage technology to communicate their vision,” said Don Mader, CEO of Southeastern. “By bringing Sunbelt into the Southeastern family, we’re expanding our ability to elevate our client’s brands creating impactful marketing and dimensional pieces that work seamlessly with their print, promo, and fulfillment programs. This acquisition accelerates our vision and enhances the value we bring to every client engagement.”

Sunbelt brings specialized expertise indimensional print, structural design, and specialty applications, expanding Southeastern’s ability to support everything from product launches and retail programs to complex kitting and distribution.

Bob Bernstein, Sunbelt Dimensional’s former CEO, added: “We’ve always been passionate about helping clients stand out through creative printing and dimensional formats. Joining Southeastern lets us plug that creativity into a larger, integrated platform—where print, promo, fulfillment, and high-end digital storefront technology work together. Our clients will benefit from expanded capabilities, deeper resources, and a broader geographic footprint.”

With the addition of Sunbelt Dimensional, Southeastern can now offer:

  • End-to-end print and packaging support for campaigns, exhibits, launches, and product programs
  • Integrated kitting and fulfillment, from printed materials to boxed goods and promo items
  • Strategic solutions for Tier 1 and high-volume brands seeking automated, data-driven tech processes, consistency and control across multiple locations and channels

“This is an exciting step in our growth journey,” Mader continued. “We’re investing in the capabilities our clients need most—so when they ask what’s next, the answer is simple: the future of print is going Southeastern.”

For more information about Southeastern’s expanded capabilities or to schedule a plant visit, please go to www.seprint.com.

About Southeastern

Southeastern Printing was founded in 1924 and has evolved to Florida’s premier enterprise partner of integrated print, dimensional, fulfillment, promotional, and creative marketing solutions for clients in healthcare, insurance, banking, tourism, retail, education, finance, and manufacturing. While adhering to leading Industry security frameworks, including HITRUST and SOC 2 certifications, Southeastern helps brands turn ideas into consistent, high impact experiences through its comprehensive capabilities in commercial printing, direct mail, packaging, promotional products, wide format, warehousing, kitting, and digital storefronts for inventory and production management.

As an industry leader in craftsmanship and continuous innovation, Southeastern delivers integrated solutions that help brands streamline their marketing operations, strengthen their presence, and accelerate their impact. The company also owns omnichannel direct mail technology BoomBox® and BrandStash®, Southeastern's online storefront and supply chain management platform, which harnesses data analytics for seamless coordination and efficient fulfillment. Additionally, SEP Communications, Southeastern's promotional products arm, was named one of South Florida's fastest growing companies in 2025 by the South Florida Business Journal due to its growing reputation as a leading strategic marketing partner in addition to offering a wide array of physical brand assets and services. Learn more at www.seprint.com

About Sunbelt

Sunbelt is a dimensional print company in operation since 1970, specializing in structural design, custom packaging, and specialty print applications. Known for its in-house engineering team and its ability to unbox packaging with highly innovative solutions, Sunbelt has supported fortune 500 clients across retail, healthcare, and luxury goods with innovative, high-quality dimensional projects that significantly elevate brand presence.

Media Contact:
Paula Mescolin
Head of Brand & Strategy
The Koolture Group
754 2158201
us@thekoolturegroup.com

data.title: Oconee Federal Financial Corp. Announces Quarterly Financial Results
data.description:
Oconee Federal Financial Corp. Announces Quarterly Financial Results

Oconee Federal Financial Corp. (OTCQX: OFED) (the “Company”), the holding company for Oconee Federal Savings and Loan Association (“Oconee Federal”), announced today net income of $1.1 million, or $0.20 per diluted share, for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, which was more than the net income of $978 thousand, or $0.17 per diluted share, for the quarter ended December 31, 2024.

December 31, 2025 Quarter End Summary:

  • Fifty-seven consecutive quarterly dividends of $0.10 per share since the quarter ended December 2011.
  • Total assets were $662.4 million, a decrease of 0.87% from $668.2 million at June 30, 2025.
  • Total loans outstanding were $485.5 million, up from $482.1 million at June 30, 2025.
  • At December 31, 2025, our stock book value and tangible book value were $15.09 and $14.64, respectively.

“The quarter ended December 31, 2025 was a good quarter for Oconee Federal. We have an increase of $162 thousand in net income when compared to the prior year’s comparable quarter. This increase was significantly helped by an improved interest margin,” stated Curtis T. Evatt, President and Chief Executive Officer. “As always, our capital ratios remain among some of the highest in the industry and we are looking forward to the opportunity of providing the best products and services to our customers in the coming year.”

Cash Dividend Declared and Paid

Total dividends paid during the quarter ended December 31, 2025 were $582 thousand. On October 23, 2025, the Board of Directors of the Company declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share of the Company’s common stock payable to stockholders of record as of November 6, 2025, which was paid on November 20, 2025.

About Oconee Federal

Oconee Federal Financial Corp. (OTCQX: OFED) is the holding company of Oconee Federal Savings and Loan Association. Oconee Federal Savings and Loan Association is a federally chartered savings and loan association founded in 1924 and headquartered in Seneca, South Carolina. Oconee Federal Savings and Loan Association is a community oriented financial institution operating eight full-service branch locations in Oconee County, South Carolina, Pickens County, South Carolina, Darlington County, South Carolina, Stephens County, Georgia and Rabun County, Georgia.

For financial information, please access our investor information page on our website at www.oconeefederal.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

This release contains certain forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 that are based on current expectations, estimates and projections about the Company’s and the Association’s industry, and management’s beliefs and assumptions. Words such as anticipates, expects, intends, plans, believes, estimates and variations of such words and expressions are intended to identify fiscal year forward-looking statements. Such statements are not guarantees of future performance and are subject to certain risks, uncertainties and assumptions that are difficult to forecast. Therefore, actual results may differ materially from those expressed or forecast in such forward-looking statements. The Company undertakes no obligation to update publicly any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information or otherwise.

Investor/Media Contact:
Curtis T. Evatt
President & Chief Executive Officer
Oconee Federal Financial Corp.
201 East North Second Street
Seneca, South Carolina
864-882-2765

data.title: Modern Wealth Expands New York Presence With $300 Million Team
data.description:
Modern Wealth Expands New York Presence With $300 Million Team

Rochester-based practice strengthens Modern Wealth’s growing East Coast presence

Modern Wealth Management (“Modern Wealth”), a registered investment advisory (RIA) firm founded to meet the evolving needs of today’s financial professionals and its clients, today announced an asset purchase agreement of a Rochester, New York-based advisory practice led by industry veteran Craig Houck. The transaction adds a four-person advisory team to Modern Wealth’s Rochester office, with Houck assuming the role of managing director.

Overseeing $300 million in client assets, Houck and his team have joined Modern Wealth to leverage its robust, fully integrated wealth management platform—expanding its ability to deliver comprehensive services including financial planning, tax planning and preparation, estate planning, investment management and more. This move further expands Modern Wealth's largest East Coast presence, which includes a growing retirement plan advisory and financial planning business, and reinforces the firm’s commitment to long-term growth in the Northeast.

“Modern Wealth provides a platform that empowers advisors to deliver exceptional client experience while accelerating their own growth,” said Houck. “In particular, the firm’s deep tax planning expertise allows us to better serve clients navigating increasingly complex financial decisions, especially as they move through retirement. Joining Modern Wealth enables us to expand our service model, collaborate with tax and planning specialists, and deliver greater value to clients with more speed and scale.”

The team brings decades of collective expertise in retirement planning, investment strategies, tax planning and estate considerations—helping clients achieve long-term financial security and navigate retirement transitions. By leveraging Modern Wealth’s Organic Growth Hub, the team will gain greater opportunity to expand within its target market. The hub delivers proven strategies for lead generation, distribution and client onboarding, alongside centralized support across marketing, technology, compliance, human resources and operations. The transition also creates structured career-path opportunities for the firm’s next generation of advisors, underscoring Modern Wealth’s commitment to talent development.

“Craig’s team’s decision to join Modern Wealth is a testament to the appeal of our approach— building strong local teams and giving advisors the resources, technology and expertise to thrive,” saidJason Gordo, President & Co-Founder of Modern Wealth. “Rochester represents our largest East Coast market, and the team strengthens our financial planning capabilities while supporting continued expansion in the region. They exemplify the type of entrepreneurial, client-focused and collaborative advisors we are proud to welcome to our platform.”

Modern Wealth continued to build strong momentum throughout 2025, adding 19 new advisory teams, expanding service lines and investing in platform capabilities with nearly $11 billion in assets under management. The firm’s continued expansion reflects increasing demand from advisors seeking resources to scale their practices while maintaining a strong local identity and client-first culture. For more information about Modern Wealth and its comprehensive wealth management services, please visit modwm.com.

About Modern Wealth Management

Modern Wealth Management is a registered investment adviser (RIA) reimagining the delivery of financial advice. Co-Founded by former United Capital executives Gary Roth, Mike Capelle and Jason Gordo, Modern Wealth was designed to anticipate the needs of Americans at every stage in life by providing a full suite of wealth management services carried out by a team of experts specializing in financial planning, tax planning and preparation, estate planning and more. Strategically acquiring high-growth RIAs across the country, Modern Wealth plans to establish regional offices in key locations spanning the United States. To learn more about Modern Wealth’s next generation platform and advice delivery model, please email info@modwm.com or visit modwm.com.

Registration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission does not imply a certain level of skill or training. Past performance is not necessarily indicative of future results.

data.title: Shop-Ware Launches VISION Experience Giveaway to Support the Next Generation of Automotive Technicians
data.description:
Shop-Ware Launches VISION Experience Giveaway to Support the Next Generation of Automotive Technicians

Community giveback contest will send one deserving automotive apprentice to VISION Hi-Tech Training & Expo in Kansas City

Shop-Ware, a cloud-based shop management platform for independent auto repair shops, today announced a new community giveback initiative called “Future Tech of the Trade – VISION Experience Giveaway” in partnership with Changing the Industry, a trusted and influential voice within the automotive community.

The giveaway will sponsor one automotive apprentice to attend the VISION Hi-Tech Training & Expo, one of the most respected and sought-after training events in the automotive industry. This year’s conference takes place March 5-8, 2026, in Kansas City. The selected apprentice will receive a full VISION experience, including event registration, travel, and lodging.

“Our industry’s future depends on the people entering it today,” said Rob Hough, EVP of the Aftermarket division at Vehlo, parent company of Shop-Ware. “This program is about investing in the future and giving back in a meaningful way by supporting apprentices who are committed to learning, growth, and craftsmanship, and helping them access world-class training that can shape their careers.”

Applications may be submitted through the official application form by apprentices themselves or by shop owners or managers nominating an apprentice. Applicants are asked to provide basic background information and short responses explaining:

  • Why they chose the automotive industry
  • What they hope to learn by attending VISION
  • What being a great technician means to them

The opportunity is open to all qualified automotive apprentices, regardless of shop affiliation. While the selected apprentice may come from a Shop-Ware customer shop, there is no requirement to be a Shop-Ware user.

Applications are open now through the end of the day Sunday, February 8. The winning apprentice will be announced on or around Friday, February 13. Final selection will be made jointly by Shop-Ware and Changing the Industry, with judging based on demonstrated passion for the trade, commitment to professional growth, and alignment with community values.

About Shop-Ware

Founded in 2013, Shop-Ware delivers professional-grade innovation to independent automotive service providers. Shop-Ware is committed to developing sophisticated tools to help the automotive aftermarket thrive and grow. Industry-first features include fully digital, cloud-based repair orders with native vehicle inspections, rich media, and customer live chat; real-time shop workflow and "expeditor" dispatch; native parts allocation and tracking for each job in progress; parts gross-profit "optimizer" that automatically seeks and attains a parts GP target; advanced analytics across unlimited repair facilities, and much more. For more information on Shop-Ware, visit shop-ware.com.

About Changing the Industry

Changing the Industry (CTI) is a media platform and podcast dedicated to elevating the voices of technicians, shop owners, and industry leaders through honest conversations, education, and community-driven initiatives. Hosted by Lucas Underwood and David Roman, CTI is a channel for shop owners to learn how to tackle the issues that come with auto shop management. Find every episode at changingtheindustrypodcast.com.

Media contact:
Mandee Smith
Mandee.Smith@vehlo.com

data.title: My wealthy friend invited me to stay, but he doesn't need more 'stuff.' What do I get the man who has everything?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'He has a beautiful home, and enough antiques and collectibles stored up over a lifetime'

"He makes a great martini, and I admire his ability to polish them off and remain upright." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

I have a dear friend who invites me to stay when I'm in town.

I usually stay for four days or so at a time, and I take him for dinner on the first night (or second night, if we don't go for dinner) and the last night too. I tell him, "That's the deal."

(We had vodka martinis the last time I visited, so we stayed home instead of going out to eat; he makes a great martini, and I admire his ability to polish them off and remain upright.)

It's hard to know what to buy. He has a beautiful home, a dog, and enough antiques and collectibles stored up over a lifetime that he doesn't need more "stuff" to clutter his home.

This begs the question: Any tips for being a good guest? Life has been good to him, and I honestly don't know what I can bring. What do you get the man who has everything?

Houseguest

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

Don't miss: Here are the most popular Moneyist columns of 2025

It's a good time to receive an extravagant gift, especially when interest rates, inflation and geopolitical unrest give us the jitters.

Dear Houseguest,

Vodka.

And books. Also, something smelly: soap, cologne, bubble bath. A photomontage of his dog (you could print out photos from social media and manually paste them into a frame). I agree that as we get older, we are often trying to rid ourselves of stuff. What he probably doesn't need are more candlesticks or china or martini glasses (although, on second thought, don't rule those out entirely; if his parties ever get raucous, there's always risk of breakages and room for more). Dinner out on the first and/or last night is also a win-win.

There's nothing more wonderful than sharing a book that you love - one that really gives you pleasure, the kind that you read again and again and believe the characters are like old friends. If he loves history, there's plenty to choose from. If he's an aficionado of fiction, there's quite a few in the latest Booker Prize shortlist. Classics are good, but not everyone is up to speed on the latest hot novel, and having read the latest book-club choices doesn't hurt for conversation at dinner parties.

At a time when geopolitical unrest gives us the jitters, it's a nice time to receive a lavish gift.

Cologne is a personal choice, true - but it's eminently regiftable, after one surreptitious spray, and it's a luxury that can feel too extravagant to purchase for oneself, especially in a climate of economic uncertainty. At a time when interest rates, inflation and geopolitical unrest give us the jitters, it's a good time to receive a seemingly lavish gift. A fancy soap and a pair of high-end socks are the kind of treats that are rarely unwelcome. Don't overlook DIY gifts either.

I'm reminded of a gift that Britain's Catherine, Princess of Wales - when she was Duchess of Cambridge - gave Queen Elizabeth II when the younger royal was a guest for her first Christmas at Sandringham. "I thought back to what I would give my own grandparents," she told a television documentary, "and I thought, 'I'll make her something.'" She gave her a homemade jar of chutney. It was a welcome gift: "I noticed the next day that it was on the table," she added.

Arguably, the late Queen Elizabeth had everything - and then some - but that homemade gift took time and effort, something that the queen clearly realized. It's a good reminder for hosts everywhere that if a guest gives you a scarf or a pair of candlesticks or place settings, or even something that is not entirely to your taste, the next time they come to stay, assuming there is a next time, it will mean a lot if they see the gift being used.

Less tangible gifts

I'm sure that I don't need to tell you this, but it doesn't hurt for us all to be reminded that value and success is an inside job. A man, woman or head of state who appears to have everything doesn't actually have everything. Material possessions aside, we never know what's going on in somebody's life, and your gift to your friend - and his gift to you - is the time that you share out of this sometimes stressful, unpredictable, wonderful and precious life.

Since you asked, your other gifts will be less tangible: Make sure your host knows when you're arriving and, if the time changes, let them know; take off your shoes when you enter somebody else's home unless you know that they wear outdoor shoes indoors (not everyone wants germs and dirt from the streets traipsed all over their carpets); offer to help with cleaning and cooking; and don't stand on ceremony or keep your nose buried in your phone.

Even if you don't care to make your bed at home, do it in your host's house.

If you've had a nightmare of a journey, don't arrive frazzled and in a bad temper. Chances are your host will be looking forward to seeing you and hearing your news, and a 20-minute recap of your flight and taxi ride should not be part of that bulletin. Even if you want to cry when you walk in the door, turn that frown upside down and greet your host with a warm hug and a smile. Smile until your cheeks hurt.

Your host may respect your privacy by not going into your bedroom without permission (even though it's their house and their room), but if they do need to pop their head around the door, a well-made bed and fluffed pillows is a mark of respect. Even if you don't care to make your bed at home, do it in your host's house. Also, if you go shopping together, take out your credit card faster than a duel in the Wild West.

It's the little, considerate gestures that your host will remember. It will give him great pleasure to see you making yourself at home in his house - but that doesn't mean lying in a bubble bath for an hour and listening to the radio while he's preparing dinner, or sitting in the garden reading the book that you brought your host while they try to fire up the barbecue. Strike that balance between the respectful and familiar.

And bring his dog a treat; after all, he loves his faithful companion, and the dog lives there too.

Related: I met a friend for lunch. When the check arrived, she said, 'Thank you so much for paying!' Was I taken for a fool?

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

I started working as a dishwasher at 13. Social Security refuses to acknowledge this. Am I being cheated?

My mom, 90, has dementia. My brother wants her to buy a $500K house near his house. Does that make any sense?

'I don't want to step on any toes': I manage an $80K trust for a 15-year-old relative. How do I deal with interfering relatives?

Check out the Moneyist private Facebook group, where we look for answers to life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms.

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  01-20-26 0900ET

data.title: How Greenland could turn into a Big Tech problem, according to Morgan Stanley's Mike Wilson
data.description:
By Barbara Kollmeyer

EU trade 'bazooka' concerns are making the rounds

Could the U.S.-EU Greenland spat put Big Tech stocks on ice. Some see a threat.

The U.S.-Europe spat over Greenland may have not been on many investor bingo cards for this year, but here we are. Wall Street looks ready to sell stocks now and ask questions later.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is telling the world not to give into hysteria but cooler heads are not at the moment prevailing. Citing potential earnings fallout, Citigroup downgraded European equities - an outperformer over the U.S. in 2025 - for the first time in a year.

Our call of the day from Morgan Stanley's chief U.S. equity strategist Mike Wilson says investors can expect a "fairly contained direct cost impact on major U.S. indices" from President Donald Trump's fresh tariff threats against the EU. He says smaller index weights, such as pockets of autos/transport equipment, staples, materials and healthcare, are most at risk.

Here's Wilson's biggest worry over the Greenland crisis: "The more notable risk is tied to whether we see the EU activate its 'anti-coercion' tool and focus on services, which would pose more of a headwind to U.S. mega caps."

He's not alone here, as Trump's EU tariff threats sparked plenty of chatter among strategists about the possibility of Europe launching a so-called "trade bazooka." Mainly seen as a deterrent measure, the Anti-Coercion Instrument dates back to 2021, when the EU was looking for a way to deter China from an economic coercion campaign against Lithuania.

"Starting the activation does not mean implementation (which requires several steps) but signals potential EU action and allows time for negotiation. The ACI could involve a range of policy tools broader than tariffs, such as investment restrictions, taxation of U.S. assets and services, like digital services, etc.," Sven Jari Stehn, chief European economist at Goldman Sachs, told clients on Monday.

The concern is that Big Tech would be an obvious heavyweight that would bear the brunt of this. Some see signs of this concern reflected in Nasdaq-100 futures, which have been driving losses for futures markets, a week away from the start of Big Tech earnings.

Christopher Granville, managing director at TS Lombard, had a similar viewpoint to share: "Market slump risk would come into play only if U.S.-Europe tensions escalated beyond tariff hikes to a more radical confrontation - with, for example, Trump weaponizing LNG exports or the EU invoking its Anti-Coercion Instrument to limit market access for Big Tech."

One group of stocks Wilson does like is small caps IWM. Even if Fed rate cut possibilities are looking a little less optimistic, with Kevin Warsh now seen as a lead candidate for Fed chair, Wilson said "fundamentals are improving and driving relative outperformance" for the small caps.

Discretionary goods, regional/mid-cap banks, shorter-cycle industrials and biotechs are Morgan Stanley's favorite small-cap sectors.

The markets

U.S. stock futures (ES00) (YM00) (NQ00) are sinking, long-dated Treasurys BX:TMUBMUSD10Y BX:TMUBMUSD30Y are selling off, not helped by soaring Japan bond yields BX:TMBMKJP-30Y BX:TMBMKJP-40Y over a tax-cut plan. Gold (GC00) and silver (SI00) are charting new highs and the dollar DXY is down. 

   Key asset performance                                                Last       5d      1m     YTD    1y 
   S&P 500                                                              6940.01    -0.38%  1.54%  1.38%  15.73%

Need to Know starts early and is updated until the opening bell, but sign up here to get it delivered once to your email box. The emailed version will be sent out at about 7:30 a.m. Eastern.

Take control of your news. Make MarketWatch your preferred source on Google.

The buzz

Trump threatened a 10% tariff against countries that deployed troops to Greenland, effective on Feb. 1. The European Union was reportedly considering countermeasures.

The U.S. Supreme Court is again planning to issue rulings on Tuesday, which means a decision on the legality of the Trump administration's tariffs could come.

3M stock (MMM) is slipping as a broad-market selloff overshadowed the Post-it Notes maker's quarterly earnings beat.Results from

Netflix (NFLX), which reports results after the close, and Warner Bros. Discovery announced a big change to their merger agreement - Netflix will make its offer all cash.

United Airlines (UAL) earnings (read a preview) are also coming after the close.

Rapt Therapeutics (RAPT) shares are jumping after GSK (GSK) announced a $2.2 billion deal to buy the maker of a food allergy drug.

Construction spending for October and pending home sales for December are expected at 10 a.m.

Best of the web

6,000 Truth Social posts later, here are the promises Trump kept - and broke.

U.K. ministers launch consultation on whether to ban social media for under 16s.

EU clings to hope it can defuse Trump at Davos.

The chart

Ben Carlson, director of institutional asset management at Ritholtz Wealth Management, shares this chart on his Wealth of Common Sense blog that offers rationale for an outperformance by international stocks last year. "The falling dollar was a tailwind for foreign stocks last year but earnings growth was good too. Everything went right for foreign stocks last year," he said. The iShares MSCI ACWI ex-U.S. ETF ACWX has gained 4% this year.

Random reads

Americans are snapping up "Make America Go Away" hats.

A sad farewell to Claude, the albino alligator.

NASA to gravity conspiracy theorists: calm down.

-Barbara Kollmeyer

data.title: Mexico's Carso to Buy Mexican Subsidiary of Lukoil
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup 




  MEXICO CITY--Mexican industrial and retail conglomerate Grupo Carso agreed to buy a subsidiary of Russia's Lukoil with which it will raise to 100% its interest in two Mexican offshore oil fields. 



  Carso, controlled by billionaire Carlos Slim, said in a filing Monday with the Mexican stock exchange that it has agreed to pay $270 million for Fieldwood Mexico, a unit of Lukoil which has a 50% interest in two oil fields in the southern Gulf of Mexico. Carso will also pay $330 million in debt that the subsidiary has with Lukoil. 



  Carso acquired its first 50% interest in the fields--Ichalkil and Pokoch--in 2024 with the acquisition of Mexico's PetroBal. 



  Lukoil is seeking to sell its international assets after being hit with U.S. sanctions in November. Carso said the transaction requires regulatory approvals in Mexico, as well as "express and specific authorization" from the U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control. 



  "With this transaction, Grupo Carso through its subsidiary Zamajal is consolidating its majority participation in the field, reaffirming its commitment to the upstream hydrocarbon sector in Mexico," the company said. 





  Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 20, 2026 08:45 ET (13:45 GMT)
